meta.note: Channel strategy. active = publishing now; suggested = engine recommendation with the evidence that would justify adopting; parked = considered and deprioritised.

items.name: LinkedIn (personal)
items.rationale: Primary channel. Where founders, commercial leaders and the events/iGaming industry already are.

items.name: X
items.rationale: Prediction markets and B2B operator conversation lives here; sharpest-sentence versions of each idea, plus threads for the bigger ones.

items.name: Substack newsletter
items.rationale: The long-form home: one idea interpreted deeply each week builds the body of thinking and an owned audience (email list is the only platform-proof asset).

items.name: LinkedIn newsletter
items.rationale: Near-free distribution: LinkedIn notifies subscribers directly. Restructure (never paste) the Substack lead for it. Grows an audience Substack can later convert.
items.watchSignal: Adopt once the Substack cadence has held for 4 consecutive weeks.

items.name: Podcast guesting circuit
items.rationale: Authority evidence that compounds: a guest slot reaches a borrowed audience and produces clips + quotes to repurpose. The outreach engine already has a podcast-pitch purpose.
items.watchSignal: Pitch 2 shows/month once 3 strong published pieces exist per target lane.

items.name: Industry trade press bylines
items.rationale: iGaming and events trade outlets take operator bylines; one placed article outranks 20 posts for credibility with sponsors and buyers. Stuart has editor relationships from the media years.
items.watchSignal: One byline per quarter; start with the lane where a published post drew trade-industry comments.

items.name: Speaking (summits, founder communities)
items.rationale: The talk-as-diagnostic format converts audiences into conversations better than any post. Singapore founder communities are cheap reps; industry summits are the compounding version.
items.watchSignal: Convert the seeded talk idea (cnt-s16) into an abstract; accept 1 community talk/month.

items.name: Short-form video (YouTube / LinkedIn native)
items.rationale: 60-90 second talking-head operator notes; LinkedIn privileges native video and it humanises the written POV. Higher production drag, so earn it with writing consistency first.
items.watchSignal: Test 4 videos after 90 days of consistent written cadence; keep only if they create conversations, not views.

items.name: Singapore founder communities (WhatsApp/Telegram/in-person)
items.rationale: Where the actual Strait Up Growth ICP asks for help. Participate, answer genuinely, never broadcast; the Tanjong-style talk loop showed this converts.
items.watchSignal: Track: replies and DMs originating from community threads (log as engagements with source).

items.name: B2B GTM/RevOps communities (Pavilion, RevGenius, Lenny-adjacent Slacks)
items.rationale: The CRM/RevOps and pricing lanes have dedicated rooms full of the exact buyer. Answer threads with operator specifics; profile does the selling.
items.watchSignal: Join 2, answer 3 threads/week for a month; keep if it produces one qualified conversation.

items.name: Substack Notes + cross-post network
items.rationale: The discovery layer for the newsletter: short takes and quotes from each issue reach other Substack readers, who convert to subscribers at high rates.
items.watchSignal: Turn 2 lines from each issue into Notes; watch subscriber source attribution.

items.name: Medium / Quora / Reddit
items.rationale: Low fit: audiences skew away from the ICP and self-promotion is punished. Only genuine answers, only if a thread lands in a lane. Do not invest.
items.watchSignal: Revisit only if search traffic to the Substack shows demand for evergreen versions.
